zh.lifeSummary: 當Catherine de la Warre於1304年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾韋斯頓 法維爾時，她的父親Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre27歲，母親Joan Grelley25歲。她於1328年之前與Warin le Latimer在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1361年8月9日享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Warre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English:  see  Warr  .
2  English:  see  Ware  .

variants.name: Warr
meanings: English (of Norman origin): nickname for a warrior, usually from Norman French were, werre, waire, warre (central French guerre) ‘war, battle’, and short for de la werre (de la guerre, see Delaware ), literally ‘(man) of the war’, a construction paralleled in de la Bataille (see Battle ). Occasionally it is a shortened form of Old French werreur ‘warrior’.
English: variant of   Ware  .

variants.name: Ware
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by or looked after a dam or weir on a river (from Old English wær, wer, Middle English war(e), warre, wer(e), werre ‘weir’), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Weare (Somerset), Weare Giffard (Devon), or Ware (Hertfordshire).
topographic name for someone who lived by a horse pond or fish pond, from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English wayour, which became early modern English ware, wear.

en.lifeSummary: When Catherine de la Warre was born in 1304, in Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre, was 27 and her mother, Joan Grelley, was 25. She married Warin le Latimer before 1328, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 9 August 1361, at the age of 57.

ko.Saint Peter's Church, Weston Favell, Wes: 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 웨스턴 파벨 스트 피터'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Catherine de la Warre(이)가 1304년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 웨스턴 파벨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Grelley(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1328년 이전 영국에서 Warin le Latimer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 8월 9일 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Catherine de la Warre nasceu em 1304, em Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Joan Grelley, tinha 25 anos. ru.Saint Peter's Church, Weston Favell, Wes: ст питир'с чурч, вестон фавелл, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Catherine de la Warre родилась в 1304 г. в местности вестон фавелл, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Grelley, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Warin le Latimer перед 1328 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 9 августа 1361 г. в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Catherine de la Warre nació en 1304, en Weston Favell, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John De La Warre 2nd Baron de la Warre, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Joan Grelley, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Warin le Latimer antes de 1328, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 9 de agosto de 1361, a la edad de 57 años.
